LAW AND MOTION, 301, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. DEFENDANT ESTHER LAU FILED YESTERDAY A PEREMPTORY 170.6 CHALLENGE TO JUDGE BUSCH. THE COURT STRIKED THE CHALLENGE AS UNTIMELY AND SHOULD BE FILED 5 DAYS IN ADVANCE OF THE HEARING. THIS IS ALSO A CONTINUATION OF A PRIOR PROCEEDING THAT IS SEEKING RECONSIDERATION OF A NEW TRIAL BASED ON A PREVIOUS RULING. THESE ISSUES ARE DECIDED BY A JUDGE WHO DECIDED THE PRIOR ISSUES. THE COURT WENT FORWARD AND HEARD THE MATTER. DEFENDANT ESTHER LAU'S INTENTION TO MOVE FOR NEW TRIAL IS DENIED. THERE IS NO NEW FACT, LAW OR REASON TO RECONSIDER. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: WENDY PUN CSR# 12891
